files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,76 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "lima-20-filesystem-sharing",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "lima: Filesystem Sharing",
+    "description": "Filesystem Sharing. Requested by 11+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current lima setup",
+        "description": "Verify your lima version and configuration:\n```bash\nlimactl --version\n```\nThis feature requires a working lima installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review lima configuration",
+        "description": "Review the relevant lima configuration:\nHello. I would like to give some ideas and advices about filesystem sharing.\n\nSo, since we are using QEMU, lets see which options do we have:\n- VirtioFS. Looking very cool, seems to have really good performance, but works only on Linux hosts."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Filesystem Sharing",
+        "description": "The guestagent will soon require root privileges (mostly for virtio stuff), so the guestagent will have to run as the root.\n```yaml\nThe error is happening because the vport device somehow wires up both `write_iter` and `write`: https://github.com/torvalds/linux/blob/v5.11/fs/read_write.c#L540-L545\n\nI guess I have to inject some pipe as a \"shim\" fd, or use ssh instead of virtserial.\n\n\nvirtserial seems slower than ssh :(\n\n\n### virtserial\nqemu flags: `-device virtio-serial -chardev socket,id=foo,path=foo.sock,server,nowait -device virtserialport,chardev=foo.sock,name=foo`\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected.\nConfirm the feature described in \"Filesystem Sharing\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "The guestagent will soon require root privileges (mostly for virtio stuff), so the guestagent will have to run as the root.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "The error is happening because the vport device somehow wires up both `write_iter` and `write`: https://github.com/torvalds/linux/blob/v5.11/fs/read_write.c#L540-L545\n\nI guess I have to inject some pipe as a \"shim\" fd, or use ssh instead of virtserial.\n\n\nvirtserial seems slower than ssh :(\n\n\n### virtserial\nqemu flags: `-device virtio-serial -chardev socket,id=foo,path=foo.sock,server,nowait -device virtserialport,chardev=foo.sock,name=foo`",
+        "2) Set up a ssh \"sync\"\n`mutagen sync create /tmp/test lima-default:/tmp/test`",
+        "With some arbitrary mapping of the mutagen YAML configuration:\n\nhttps://mutagen.io/documentation/introduction/configuration#configuration-files"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "lima",
+      "incubating",
+      "app-definition",
+      "feature"
+    ],
+    "cncfProjects": [
+      "lima"
+    ],

+    "difficulty": "advanced",

+    ],

+    },

+  },

+      "limactl"
+    ],

+  },
